Given: A registered user is ready to login
When: The user enters valid email and password
Then: The system prompts for TFA, sends OTP to registered contact, and requires OTP verification before granting access

Given: An OTP has been sent to the user's registered contact
When: The user receives and enters the correct OTP
Then: The system validates the OTP, grants access to the account, and establishes a secure session

Given: A user is logged in to their account
When: The user logs out and attempts to login again with valid credentials
Then: The system again prompts for TFA authentication immediately, requiring new OTP verification

Given: An OTP has been sent to a user
When: The user requests a new OTP before expiration
Then: The system generates a new OTP, invalidates the previous OTP, and sends the new OTP

Given: An OTP has been sent and is valid
When: The user enters an incorrect OTP
Then: The system displays an error message, denies access, and allows the user to request a new OTP

Given: The OTP prompt is displayed on the login form
When: The user submits the form without entering any OTP
Then: The system displays a validation error message stating OTP field is mandatory

Given: The OTP input field expects numeric values
When: The user enters alphabetic or special characters in the OTP field
Then: The system rejects the input, displays an error, and prompts for numeric-only input

Given: An OTP has been sent to the user
When: The user attempts to enter the OTP after expiration window (5-10 minutes)
Then: The system rejects the OTP as expired, displays an error message, and prompts the user to request a new OTP

Given: A user has received an OTP
When: The user enters an incorrect OTP multiple times (3+ failed attempts)
Then: The system either locks the attempt temporarily or prompts for alternative authentication method

Given: An OTP input field is displayed
When: The user enters an OTP longer than the maximum expected length (e.g.
Related_AC: The system either truncates or rejects the input with an error message

Given: An OTP input field is displayed
When: The user enters an OTP shorter than the minimum required length
Then: The system displays an error and prompts for the correct length OTP

Given: An OTP input field is displayed
When: The user enters an OTP with leading or trailing spaces
Then: The system should strip whitespace and validate the OTP correctly

Given: A user has successfully entered a valid OTP
When: The system verifies the OTP successfully
Then: The user is granted access to their account and session tokens are generated and stored

Scenario_Name: TFA Applies to All User Types
Given: Users with different roles (admin, user, manager) are attempting to login
Then: TFA is required for all user types without exception before granting access

Given: The system and OTP service have misaligned timestamps by more than acceptable tolerance
When: A user attempts to validate an OTP
Then: The system either resynchronizes clocks or rejects the OTP with guidance to contact support
